test_cases.description: Verifies the workspaces table DDL defines all required columns with correct types and constraints.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised by running the DDL in internal/db/db.go against a fresh in-memory database
The teams table exists (team_rename spec 06 migration has run)
test_cases.input: PRAGMA table_info('workspaces')
test_cases.expected: Columns returned: id (TEXT), slug (TEXT), git_url (TEXT), branch (TEXT nullable), owner_id (TEXT), team_id (TEXT nullable), status (TEXT), created_at (DATETIME); id is PRIMARY KEY
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cols = db.query("PRAGMA table_info('workspaces')")
assert col_named(cols, 'id').type == 'TEXT'
assert col_named(cols, 'slug').type == 'TEXT' AND col_named(cols, 'slug').notnull == 1
assert col_named(cols, 'git_url').type == 'TEXT' AND col_named(cols, 'git_url').notnull == 1
assert col_named(cols, 'branch').notnull == 0
assert col_named(cols, 'owner_id').type == 'TEXT' AND col_named(cols, 'owner_id').notnull == 1
assert col_named(cols, 'team_id').notnull == 0
assert col_named(cols, 'status').type == 'TEXT' AND col_named(cols, 'status').notnull == 1
assert col_named(cols, 'created_at').type == 'DATETIME' AND col_named(cols, 'created_at').notnull == 1

test_cases.description: Verifies the UNIQUE constraint on slug and NOT NULL constraints are enforced at the database layer.
preconditions: SQLite in-memory database initialised with schema from internal/db/db.go
A workspace row with slug='test-ws' and all NOT NULL fields populated has been inserted
test_cases.input: INSERT INTO workspaces (id, slug, git_url, owner_id, status, created_at) VALUES ('uuid-2', 'test-ws', 'https://github.com/org/repo.git', 'user-uuid', 'active', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
test_cases.expected: SQLite returns UNIQUE constraint failed: workspaces.slug error; original row is unmodified
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
err = db.exec("INSERT INTO workspaces (id, slug, git_url, owner_id, status, created_at) VALUES ('uuid-2', 'test-ws', 'https://github.com/org/repo.git', 'user-uuid', 'active', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)")
assert err contains 'UNIQUE constraint failed'
assert db.query_one("SELECT count(*) FROM workspaces WHERE slug='test-ws'") == 1

test_cases.description: Verifies that status defaults to 'active' and created_at defaults to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP when not explicitly provided.
preconditions: SQLite in-memory database initialised with schema from internal/db/db.go
A valid user row exists in users table with id='user-uuid'
test_cases.input: INSERT INTO workspaces (id, slug, git_url, owner_id) VALUES ('uuid-1', 'my-ws', 'https://github.com/org/repo.git', 'user-uuid')
test_cases.expected: Row inserted successfully; status column equals 'active'; created_at is non-null and approximately equal to the current UTC time
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.exec("INSERT INTO workspaces (id, slug, git_url, owner_id) VALUES ('uuid-1', 'my-ws', 'https://github.com/org/repo.git', 'user-uuid')")
row = db.query_one("SELECT status, created_at FROM workspaces WHERE id='uuid-1'")
assert row.status == 'active'
assert row.created_at != NULL
assert parse_time(row.created_at) is approximately now (within 5 seconds)

test_cases.description: Verifies CreateWorkspace inserts a workspace record and returns a fully populated Workspace struct with auto-generated UUID and defaults.
preconditions: SQLite in-memory database initialised with schema from internal/db/db.go
Valid user row in users table with id='user-uuid-1'
Workspace store is instantiated and connected to the test database
test_cases.input: CreateWorkspace(slug='new-ws', git_url='https://github.com/org/repo.git', branch=NULL, owner_id='user-uuid-1', team_id=NULL)
test_cases.expected: Returns (*Workspace, nil); Workspace.id is a valid UUID string; Workspace.status == 'active'; Workspace.created_at is non-zero; Workspace.slug == 'new-ws'; Workspace.git_url == 'https://github.com/org/repo.git'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ws, err = store.CreateWorkspace({slug: 'new-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo.git', branch: NULL, owner_id: 'user-uuid-1', team_id: NULL})
assert err == nil
assert ws != nil
assert is_valid_uuid(ws.id)
assert ws.status == 'active'
assert ws.created_at is not zero
assert ws.slug == 'new-ws'
assert ws.git_url == 'https://github.com/org/repo.git'
assert ws.branch == NULL
assert ws.team_id == NULL

test_cases.description: Verifies CreateWorkspace returns a distinct typed duplicate-slug error (not a generic error) when a slug already exists.
preconditions: SQLite in-memory database initialised with schema from internal/db/db.go
A workspace with slug='existing-ws' already exists in the workspaces table
Workspace store instantiated
test_cases.input: CreateWorkspace(slug='existing-ws', git_url='https://github.com/org/repo.git', owner_id='user-uuid-1')
test_cases.expected: Returns (nil, ErrDuplicateSlug) where ErrDuplicateSlug is a sentinel typed error distinguishable from generic database errors; no new row is inserted
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ws, err = store.CreateWorkspace({slug: 'existing-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo.git', owner_id: 'user-uuid-1'})
assert ws == nil
assert err != nil
assert is_duplicate_slug_error(err) == true
assert db.query_one("SELECT count(*) FROM workspaces WHERE slug='existing-ws'") == 1

test_cases.description: Verifies the workspace store never calls os.Exit or log.Fatal by always returning errors as Go error values.
preconditions: Workspace store is instantiated with a database connection that will fail on insert (e.g. read-only database)
test_cases.input: CreateWorkspace(slug='any-ws', git_url='https://github.com/org/repo.git', owner_id='user-uuid-1')
test_cases.expected: Function returns (nil, non-nil error); the process does not exit; no os.Exit or log.Fatal is called

test_cases.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 201 with a fully populated workspace JSON object on a valid authenticated request.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server started with the POST /api/v1/workspaces route registered
SQLite database initialised with schema from internal/db/db.go
A user record exists in users table with id='user-uuid-1'
A valid API key for user 'user-uuid-1' is configured in the auth system
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with header 'Authorization: Bearer <valid-api-key>', body: {"slug": "my-api", "git_url": "https://github.com/org/repo.git", "branch": "main"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response body is valid JSON containing: id (UUID), slug='my-api', git_url='https://github.com/org/repo.git', branch='main', owner_id='user-uuid-1', team_id=null, status='active', created_at (non-empty timestamp)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer valid-api-key'}, body={slug:'my-api', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', branch:'main'})
assert resp.status_code == 201
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert is_valid_uuid(body.id)
assert body.slug == 'my-api'
assert body.git_url == 'https://github.com/org/repo.git'
assert body.branch == 'main'
assert body.owner_id == 'user-uuid-1'
assert body.team_id == null
assert body.status == 'active'
assert body.created_at != ''

test_cases.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 400 with 'missing required fields' when slug or git_url is absent.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered
Valid API key for a non-admin user is available
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with valid auth header, body: {"git_url": "https://github.com/org/repo.git"} (missing slug)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body: {"error": {"code": "400", "message": "missing required fields"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer valid-api-key'}, body={git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert resp.status_code == 400
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error.code == '400'
assert body.error.message == 'missing required fields'
// Also test missing git_url
resp2 = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer valid-api-key'}, body={slug:'my-ws'})
assert resp2.status_code == 400
assert parse_json(resp2.body).error.message == 'missing required fields'

test_cases.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 400 with 'invalid slug format' when the slug does not match the required format.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered
Valid API key for a non-admin user is available
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with valid auth header, body: {"slug": "1-bad-slug", "git_url": "https://github.com/org/repo.git"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body: {"error": {"code": "400", "message": "invalid slug format"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer valid-api-key'}, body={slug:'1-bad-slug', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert resp.status_code == 400
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error.code == '400'
assert body.error.message == 'invalid slug format'

test_cases.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 400 with 'invalid git_url format' when the git_url is not a valid HTTPS or SSH URL.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered
Valid API key for a non-admin user is available
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with valid auth header, body: {"slug": "my-api", "git_url": "/local/path/repo"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body: {"error": {"code": "400", "message": "invalid git_url format"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer valid-api-key'}, body={slug:'my-api', git_url:'/local/path/repo'})
assert resp.status_code == 400
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error.code == '400'
assert body.error.message == 'invalid git_url format'

test_cases.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 409 with 'workspace slug already exists' when a duplicate slug is submitted.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered
SQLite database has a workspace row with slug='existing-ws'
Valid API key for a non-admin user is available
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with valid auth header, body: {"slug": "existing-ws", "git_url": "https://github.com/org/other.git"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 409; response body: {"error": {"code": "409", "message": "workspace slug already exists"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer valid-api-key'}, body={slug:'existing-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/other.git'})
assert resp.status_code == 409
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error.code == '409'
assert body.error.message == 'workspace slug already exists'

test_cases.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 404 with 'team not found' when a non-existent team_id is provided.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered
SQLite database has no team with id='nonexistent-team-uuid'
Valid API key for a non-admin user is available
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with valid auth header, body: {"slug": "my-api", "git_url": "https://github.com/org/repo.git", "team_id": "nonexistent-team-uuid"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404; response body: {"error": {"code": "404", "message": "team not found"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer valid-api-key'}, body={slug:'my-api', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', team_id:'nonexistent-team-uuid'})
assert resp.status_code == 404
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error.code == '404'
assert body.error.message == 'team not found'

test_cases.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 403 with 'not a member of this team' when the authenticated user is not a member of the specified team.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered
SQLite database has a team with id='team-uuid-1'
User 'user-uuid-1' is NOT a member of team 'team-uuid-1'
Valid API key for user 'user-uuid-1' is available
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with user-uuid-1 auth header, body: {"slug": "my-api", "git_url": "https://github.com/org/repo.git", "team_id": "team-uuid-1"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403; response body: {"error": {"code": "403", "message": "not a member of this team"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer user-uuid-1-api-key'}, body={slug:'my-api', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', team_id:'team-uuid-1'})
assert resp.status_code == 403
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error.code == '403'
assert body.error.message == 'not a member of this team'

test_cases.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 403 with 'workspace creation requires user authentication' when an admin token is used.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered
A valid admin token (not associated with a real user) is available
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with admin token auth header, body: {"slug": "my-api", "git_url": "https://github.com/org/repo.git"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403; response body: {"error": {"code": "403", "message": "workspace creation requires user authentication"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer admin-token'}, body={slug:'my-api', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert resp.status_code == 403
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error.code == '403'
assert body.error.message == 'workspace creation requires user authentication'

test_cases.description: Verifies that owner_id is set from the authenticated user's identity and cannot be supplied by the request body.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered
User 'user-uuid-1' exists in users table
Valid API key for user 'user-uuid-1' is available
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with user-uuid-1 auth header, body: {"slug": "my-api", "git_url": "https://github.com/org/repo.git", "owner_id": "attacker-uuid"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response body has owner_id == 'user-uuid-1' (the authenticated user's ID), not 'attacker-uuid'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer user-uuid-1-api-key'}, body={slug:'my-api', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', owner_id:'attacker-uuid'})
assert resp.status_code == 201
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.owner_id == 'user-uuid-1'
assert body.owner_id != 'attacker-uuid'

test_cases.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces stores NULL for branch and team_id when they are omitted, and reflects null in the response JSON.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered
Valid API key for a non-admin user is available
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with valid auth header, body: {"slug": "no-branch-ws", "git_url": "https://github.com/org/repo.git"} (branch and team_id omitted)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response body has branch=null and team_id=null; the workspaces row in SQLite has NULL for both columns
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer valid-api-key'}, body={slug:'no-branch-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert resp.status_code == 201
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.branch == null
assert body.team_id == null
row = db.query_one("SELECT branch, team_id FROM workspaces WHERE slug='no-branch-ws'")
assert row.branch == NULL
assert row.team_id == NULL

test_cases.description: Verifies the afc workspace create command is registered as a Cobra subcommand with required flags --git-url and --slug, and optional flags --branch and --team.
preconditions: The afc CLI binary is built from cmd/afc/main.go (or equivalent entry point)
Cobra command tree is initialised
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --help
test_cases.expected: Help output shows --git-url (required), --slug (required), --branch (optional), --team (optional); command is listed under 'afc workspace'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cmd = find_cobra_command(root_cmd, ['workspace', 'create'])
assert cmd != nil
assert cmd.flag('git-url') != nil AND cmd.flag('git-url').required == true
assert cmd.flag('slug') != nil AND cmd.flag('slug').required == true
assert cmd.flag('branch') != nil AND cmd.flag('branch').required == false
assert cmd.flag('team') != nil AND cmd.flag('team').required == false

test_cases.description: Verifies that when --team is provided, the CLI calls GET /api/v1/teams with a slug filter to resolve to a UUID before calling POST /api/v1/workspaces.
preconditions: A mock or test HTTP server is available that responds to GET /api/v1/teams?slug=my-team with a team record containing id='team-uuid-1'
The mock server also accepts POST /api/v1/workspaces
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-ws --team my-team
test_cases.expected: GET /api/v1/teams?slug=my-team is called first; POST /api/v1/workspaces body contains team_id='team-uuid-1'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
requests_captured = []
mock_server.on('GET', '/api/v1/teams', respond_with={teams:[{id:'team-uuid-1', slug:'my-team'}]})
mock_server.on('POST', '/api/v1/workspaces', respond_with=201_created_body, capture_request=true)
run_cli('workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-ws --team my-team')
assert requests_captured[0].method == 'GET'
assert 'slug=my-team' in requests_captured[0].url
create_request_body = parse_json(requests_captured[1].body)
assert create_request_body.team_id == 'team-uuid-1'

test_cases.description: Verifies the CLI prints the created workspace object as JSON to stdout and exits with code 0 on a successful POST /api/v1/workspaces call.
preconditions: A mock HTTP server responds to POST /api/v1/workspaces with HTTP 201 and a valid workspace JSON body
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-ws
test_cases.expected: stdout contains a valid JSON workspace object with expected fields; process exits with code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
stdout, stderr, exit_code = run_cli_capture('workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-ws')
assert exit_code == 0
body = parse_json(stdout)
assert body.slug == 'my-ws'
assert body.git_url == 'https://github.com/org/repo.git'
assert is_valid_uuid(body.id)

test_cases.description: Verifies the CLI prints an error to stderr and exits with code 1 (without calling POST /api/v1/workspaces) when the team slug cannot be resolved.
preconditions: A mock HTTP server responds to GET /api/v1/teams?slug=unknown-team with an empty teams list or 404
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-ws --team unknown-team
test_cases.expected: stderr contains a message about the unresolved team slug 'unknown-team'; exit code is 1; POST /api/v1/workspaces is never called
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
post_called = false
mock_server.on('GET', '/api/v1/teams', respond_with={teams:[]})
mock_server.on('POST', '/api/v1/workspaces', callback=lambda: set post_called=true)
stdout, stderr, exit_code = run_cli_capture('workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-ws --team unknown-team')
assert exit_code != 0
assert 'unknown-team' in stderr
assert post_called == false

test_cases.description: Verifies the CLI prints the error message from the standard error envelope to stderr and exits with code 1 when POST /api/v1/workspaces returns an error.
preconditions: A mock HTTP server responds to POST /api/v1/workspaces with HTTP 409 and body: {"error": {"code": "409", "message": "workspace slug already exists"}}
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug existing-ws
test_cases.expected: stderr contains 'workspace slug already exists'; exit code is non-zero (1)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
stdout, stderr, exit_code = run_cli_capture('workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug existing-ws')
assert exit_code != 0
assert 'workspace slug already exists' in stderr

test_cases.description: Verifies Cobra prints usage help and error message to stderr and exits with code 1 when a required flag is missing, before any API calls are made.
preconditions: The afc CLI binary is available
No mock server is started
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git (missing --slug)
test_cases.expected: stderr contains usage text mentioning --slug is required; exit code is non-zero; no HTTP requests are made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
stdout, stderr, exit_code = run_cli_capture('workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git')
assert exit_code != 0
assert 'required' in stderr OR 'slug' in stderr
// Verify by not starting any mock server — no panic or connection error from HTTP call

test_cases.description: Verifies the afc workspace create command handler code does not directly call os.Exit.
preconditions: Source files for the workspace create command handler are available (e.g. cmd/afc/workspace.go or equivalent)
test_cases.input: Static analysis / grep of handler source files
test_cases.expected: No direct calls to os.Exit in the command handler or library code; process termination is only via Cobra's command runner

test_cases.description: Verifies all architecture and docs files replace 'workspace' (referring to organizational/team concept) with 'team'.
preconditions: The architecture/ and docs/ directories exist in the repository
test_cases.input: All .md files in architecture/ and docs/ directories
test_cases.expected: No occurrence of the word 'workspace' (case-insensitive) remains when it is used to describe the organizational/team concept; all such references have been updated to 'team'

test_cases.description: Verifies architecture/services-architecture.md describes workspace as a git-repository-mapped entity with owner_id, optional team_id, optional branch, and status.
preconditions: architecture/services-architecture.md exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Content of architecture/services-architecture.md
test_cases.expected: File contains a workspace definition section that mentions: maps to a git repository, optional branch, owner_id (owned by a user), team_id (optional team association), status (active/archived)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = read_file('architecture/services-architecture.md')
assert 'git' in content.lower() OR 'repository' in content.lower()
assert 'owner_id' in content
assert 'team_id' in content
assert 'branch' in content
assert 'active' in content AND 'archived' in content

test_cases.description: Verifies architecture/services-architecture.md operational store schema section includes both the teams table and the new workspaces table with all required columns.
preconditions: architecture/services-architecture.md exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Content of architecture/services-architecture.md
test_cases.expected: Both 'teams' and 'workspaces' table schemas are present in the schema section; workspaces columns include: id, slug, git_url, branch, owner_id, team_id, status, created_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = read_file('architecture/services-architecture.md')
assert 'teams' in content
assert 'workspaces' in content
for col in ['id', 'slug', 'git_url', 'branch', 'owner_id', 'team_id', 'status', 'created_at']:
    assert col in content, f'Column {col} missing from architecture doc'

test_cases.description: Verifies the slug validator accepts slugs that start with a letter and contain only lowercase alphanumeric characters and hyphens, with length 3–64.
preconditions: Slug validator function is available (e.g. isValidSlug or validateSlug)
test_cases.input: Valid slug values: 'abc', 'my-api', 'a12', 'a-b-c', 'my-workspace-123', a string of 64 lowercase alphanumeric chars starting with a letter
test_cases.expected: Validator returns true for all valid inputs
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert validateSlug('abc') == true
assert validateSlug('my-api') == true
assert validateSlug('a12') == true
assert validateSlug('a-b-c') == true
assert validateSlug('my-workspace-123') == true
assert validateSlug('a' + repeat('b', 63)) == true  // 64 chars

test_cases.description: Verifies the slug validator rejects slugs starting with a digit or hyphen, containing uppercase letters, consecutive hyphens, trailing hyphens, or with length outside 3–64.
preconditions: Slug validator function is available
test_cases.input: Invalid slug values: '1abc', '-abc', 'MyApi', 'my--api', 'my-api-', 'ab' (2 chars), '' (empty), a 65-char string
test_cases.expected: Validator returns false for all invalid inputs
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert validateSlug('1abc') == false  // starts with digit
assert validateSlug('-abc') == false  // starts with hyphen
assert validateSlug('MyApi') == false  // uppercase
assert validateSlug('my--api') == false  // consecutive hyphens
assert validateSlug('my-api-') == false  // trailing hyphen
assert validateSlug('ab') == false  // too short
assert validateSlug(repeat('a', 65)) == false  // too long

test_cases.description: Verifies the git_url validator accepts HTTPS URLs starting with https:// and SSH URLs matching git@host:path pattern.
preconditions: git_url validator function is available (e.g. isValidGitURL or validateGitURL)
test_cases.input: Valid URLs: 'https://github.com/org/repo.git', 'https://gitlab.com/user/project', 'git@github.com:org/repo.git', 'git@bitbucket.org:user/repo.git'
test_cases.expected: Validator returns true for all valid inputs
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert validateGitURL('https://github.com/org/repo.git') == true
assert validateGitURL('https://gitlab.com/user/project') == true
assert validateGitURL('git@github.com:org/repo.git') == true
assert validateGitURL('git@bitbucket.org:user/repo.git') == true

test_cases.description: Verifies the git_url validator rejects local filesystem paths, http://, git://, ssh:// schemes, and any other non-HTTPS/SSH format.
preconditions: git_url validator function is available
test_cases.input: Invalid URLs: '/local/path/repo', 'http://github.com/repo.git', 'git://github.com/repo.git', 'ssh://git@github.com/repo.git', 'ftp://example.com/repo'
test_cases.expected: Validator returns false for all invalid inputs
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert validateGitURL('/local/path/repo') == false
assert validateGitURL('http://github.com/repo.git') == false
assert validateGitURL('git://github.com/repo.git') == false
assert validateGitURL('ssh://git@github.com/repo.git') == false
assert validateGitURL('ftp://example.com/repo') == false

test_cases.description: Verifies the git_url validator performs no network calls — validation is purely syntactic.
preconditions: git_url validator function is available
Network access is blocked (e.g. no network interface, or using a network namespace with no external access)
test_cases.input: validateGitURL('https://unreachable.example.com/repo.git')
test_cases.expected: Function returns true (syntactically valid HTTPS URL) without making any network calls; completes in well under 1 millisecond
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// Intercept/monitor network calls
net_calls_before = count_network_syscalls()
result = validateGitURL('https://unreachable.example.com/repo.git')
net_calls_after = count_network_syscalls()
assert result == true
assert net_calls_after == net_calls_before  // no network syscalls made

property_tests.description: For any sequence of CreateWorkspace calls with arbitrary slugs, no two successfully inserted workspace records in the workspaces table share the same slug value.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate a list of 2–50 slug strings (some duplicates, some unique); call CreateWorkspace for each in sequence.
property_tests.invariant_check: After all calls, query SELECT slug, count(*) FROM workspaces GROUP BY slug HAVING count(*) > 1; assert the result set is empty.

property_tests.description: For any workspace record inserted via CreateWorkspace, owner_id always references an existing row in the users table and was derived from an authenticated non-admin user's API key.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary valid workspace creation inputs (slug, git_url) paired with authenticated non-admin user API keys; call POST /api/v1/workspaces.
property_tests.invariant_check: For each created workspace row, assert: SELECT count(*) FROM users WHERE id = workspace.owner_id returns 1; assert owner_id was never supplied by the request body but derived from the auth context.

property_tests.description: For any newly created workspace record, status is always 'active' and never null immediately after creation.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary valid CreateWorkspace inputs (varying slug, git_url, branch, team_id); call CreateWorkspace for each.
property_tests.invariant_check: For each returned Workspace struct, assert ws.status == 'active'; also query database: SELECT status FROM workspaces WHERE id = ws.id and assert result == 'active' and NOT NULL.

property_tests.description: For any workspace record in workspaces with a non-null team_id, the owner_id user was a member of that team at the time the workspace was created.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate pairs of (user, team) where user is/is not a member; attempt POST /api/v1/workspaces with team_id for each combination.
property_tests.invariant_check: For all successfully created workspaces with non-null team_id: assert a team_membership row exists for (owner_id, team_id) with any role. For all rejected requests (non-member): assert no workspace row was inserted.

property_tests.description: For any workspace record stored in the workspaces table, git_url always matches the HTTPS or SSH pattern and is never null or empty.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate a mix of valid (HTTPS and SSH) and invalid git_url strings; attempt CreateWorkspace via the REST endpoint for each.
property_tests.invariant_check: For every row in workspaces: assert git_url IS NOT NULL; assert git_url != ''; assert git_url starts with 'https://' OR matches regex '^git@[^:]+:[^/].*'.

property_tests.description: For any workspace record stored in the workspaces table, slug always matches the required pattern (starts with [a-z], lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, length 3–64) and is never null.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate a mix of valid and invalid slug strings; attempt CreateWorkspace via the REST endpoint for each.
property_tests.invariant_check: For every row in workspaces: assert slug IS NOT NULL; assert slug matches regex '^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{1,62}[a-z0-9]$' OR is exactly 3 chars starting with [a-z]; assert len(slug) >= 3 AND len(slug) <= 64.

property_tests.description: For any call to the workspace store (including error paths), the store always returns an error value and never terminates the process via os.Exit or log.Fatal, and the call is bounded (completes without infinite looping).
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary workspace store inputs including boundary conditions (locked DB, duplicate slug, invalid FK, valid inputs); call CreateWorkspace for each.
property_tests.invariant_check: Each call to CreateWorkspace must: (1) return within a bounded time (< 5 seconds); (2) return a Go error value or nil — never hang or panic; (3) not invoke os.Exit or log.Fatal (verified by static analysis or test double). Assert loop/retry count == 1 for all error-path calls.

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies the database rejects a duplicate slug INSERT with a UNIQUE constraint violation and leaves the existing row unmodified.
preconditions: SQLite in-memory database initialised with schema from internal/db/db.go
A workspace row with slug='dup-ws', id='uuid-1' exists in workspaces
edge_case_tests.input: INSERT INTO workspaces (id, slug, git_url, owner_id, status, created_at) VALUES ('uuid-2', 'dup-ws', 'https://github.com/org/repo.git', 'user-uuid', 'active', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
edge_case_tests.expected: Error returned matching 'UNIQUE constraint failed'; count of rows with slug='dup-ws' remains 1; original row with id='uuid-1' is unchanged
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
err = db.exec("INSERT INTO workspaces (id, slug, git_url, owner_id, status, created_at) VALUES ('uuid-2', 'dup-ws', 'https://github.com/org/repo.git', 'user-uuid', 'active', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)")
assert err != nil AND 'UNIQUE constraint failed' in err.message
assert db.query_one("SELECT count(*) FROM workspaces WHERE slug='dup-ws'") == 1
assert db.query_one("SELECT id FROM workspaces WHERE slug='dup-ws'") == 'uuid-1'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies DDL creation of the workspaces table fails or is blocked when the teams table does not exist, preventing application startup.
preconditions: A completely fresh SQLite in-memory database with no tables
The team_rename spec (06) migration has NOT been applied (no teams table)
edge_case_tests.input: Execute workspaces table CREATE DDL from internal/db/db.go without first creating the teams table
edge_case_tests.expected: Database returns a foreign key reference error or the application startup function returns a non-nil error; process does not enter the serving state

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies CreateWorkspace returns a wrapped database error without retrying when the SQLite database is locked or unavailable.
preconditions: Workspace store instantiated with a database that will return a 'database is locked' error on any write operation
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns (nil, error) where error wraps or contains database lock information; no retry is attempted; the workspaces table has no new row
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
db_mock = new_locked_db()
store = new_workspace_store(db_mock)
ws, err = store.CreateWorkspace({slug:'any-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', owner_id:'user-uuid-1'})
assert ws == nil
assert err != nil
assert 'locked' in err.message OR 'unavailable' in err.message
// Confirm single attempt: mock should record exactly 1 write call
assert db_mock.write_call_count == 1

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies CreateWorkspace returns a foreign key constraint error without inserting a workspace row when team_id references a non-existent team.
preconditions: SQLite in-memory database initialised with schema from internal/db/db.go and PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON
No team with id='nonexistent-team' in the teams table
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns (nil, error) where error indicates foreign key violation; workspaces table has no row with slug='fk-test-ws'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
ws, err = store.CreateWorkspace({slug:'fk-test-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', owner_id:'user-uuid-1', team_id:'nonexistent-team'})
assert ws == nil
assert err != nil
assert 'foreign key' in err.message.lower() OR 'constraint' in err.message.lower()
assert db.query_one("SELECT count(*) FROM workspaces WHERE slug='fk-test-ws'") == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 400 with 'missing required fields' without hitting the database when the request body is malformed JSON.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered
Valid API key for a non-admin user is available
Database query counter is set to 0
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with valid auth header, Content-Type: application/json, body: '{invalid-json}'
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; response body: {"error": {"code": "400", "message": "missing required fields"}}; database write call count remains 0
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer valid-api-key', 'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, raw_body='{invalid-json}')
assert resp.status_code == 400
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error.code == '400'
assert body.error.message == 'missing required fields'
assert db.write_call_count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies the handler returns HTTP 500 and logs the timeout without leaking goroutines when the CreateWorkspace database call takes longer than the configured request timeout.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with a short request timeout (e.g. 100ms)
Database is configured to block all writes for at least 1 second
Valid API key for a non-admin user is available
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with valid auth header, body: {"slug": "timeout-ws", "git_url": "https://github.com/org/repo.git"}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500 with standard error envelope; no goroutines are leaked (goroutine count stable before/after); server continues to handle subsequent requests
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
goroutine_count_before = runtime.num_goroutines()
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer valid-api-key'}, body={slug:'timeout-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert resp.status_code == 500
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error != nil
sleep(200ms)  // let goroutines settle
assert runtime.num_goroutines() <= goroutine_count_before + 1

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 401 when no API key header is present in the request.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server running with POST /api/v1/workspaces registered and authentication middleware active
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with no Authorization header, body: {"slug": "my-ws", "git_url": "https://github.com/org/repo.git"}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 401; response body uses standard error envelope; the workspace create handler logic is never reached
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={}, body={slug:'my-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert resp.status_code == 401
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error != nil
// Confirm handler not reached: db write count == 0
assert db.write_call_count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies the CLI prints a human-readable network error to stderr and exits with code 1 without retrying when GET /api/v1/teams times out during team slug resolution.
preconditions: A mock server that hangs on GET /api/v1/teams (simulating timeout)
CLI is configured with a short HTTP timeout (e.g. 1 second)
edge_case_tests.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-ws --team some-team
edge_case_tests.expected: stderr contains a description of the network/timeout failure; exit code is non-zero (1); POST /api/v1/workspaces is never called
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
post_called = false
mock_server.on('GET', '/api/v1/teams', delay=10s)  // simulate timeout
mock_server.on('POST', '/api/v1/workspaces', callback=lambda: set post_called=true)
stdout, stderr, exit_code = run_cli_capture('workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-ws --team some-team')
assert exit_code != 0
assert len(stderr) > 0  // some network error description
assert post_called == false

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies the CLI prints a human-readable network error to stderr and exits with code 1 when POST /api/v1/workspaces times out.
preconditions: A mock server that responds normally to GET /api/v1/teams but hangs on POST /api/v1/workspaces
CLI configured with a short HTTP timeout
edge_case_tests.expected: stderr contains a description of the POST network/timeout failure; exit code is non-zero (1)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mock_server.on('POST', '/api/v1/workspaces', delay=10s)
stdout, stderr, exit_code = run_cli_capture('workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-ws')
assert exit_code != 0
assert len(stderr) > 0  // describes POST failure

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies the CLI prints a descriptive parse error to stderr and exits with code 1 when the API response body cannot be parsed as valid JSON.
preconditions: A mock server responds to POST /api/v1/workspaces with HTTP 200 but body: 'not-json-at-all'
edge_case_tests.expected: stderr contains a message describing the JSON parse failure or unexpected server response; exit code is non-zero (1)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mock_server.on('POST', '/api/v1/workspaces', respond_with={status:200, body:'not-json-at-all'})
stdout, stderr, exit_code = run_cli_capture('workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-ws')
assert exit_code != 0
assert 'parse' in stderr.lower() OR 'invalid' in stderr.lower() OR 'unexpected' in stderr.lower() OR 'json' in stderr.lower()

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies the slug validator returns false without panicking when an empty string is passed.
preconditions: Slug validator function is available
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns false; no panic or runtime error occurs

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies the git_url validator returns false without panicking when an empty string is passed.
preconditions: git_url validator function is available
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns false; no panic or runtime error occurs

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the happy-path workspace creation via REST API: valid API key, slug, git_url, optional branch and team_id, results in HTTP 201 with complete workspace JSON.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send POST /api/v1/workspaces with a valid non-admin API key, JSON body containing slug='smoke-api', git_url='https://github.com/org/repo.git', branch='main', and a valid team_id for a team the user belongs to.
expected_effects: HTTP 201 response with JSON body containing id (UUID), slug='smoke-api', git_url='https://github.com/org/repo.git', branch='main', owner_id (authenticated user's ID), team_id (provided team UUID), status='active', created_at (non-empty timestamp)
A new row exists in the workspaces SQLite table with matching values
The workspaces table row has status='active' and created_at is approximately the current UTC time

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for end-to-end workspace creation via the CLI with team slug resolution: CLI resolves team slug to UUID via GET /api/v1/teams then calls POST /api/v1/workspaces.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug cli-ws --team existing-team-slug (with a valid API key configured in CLI environment).
real_components: afc CLI binary (workspace create Cobra command)
Echo HTTP server (GET /api/v1/teams handler and POST /api/v1/workspaces handler)
expected_effects: GET /api/v1/teams with slug filter is called first and returns the team's UUID
POST /api/v1/workspaces is called with team_id set to the resolved UUID
CLI stdout contains the created workspace JSON object with slug='cli-ws' and team_id equal to the resolved UUID
A new row exists in the workspaces SQLite table with slug='cli-ws' and the correct team_id

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for slug conflict rejection: POST /api/v1/workspaces with a duplicate slug returns HTTP 409 with the standard error envelope.
smoke_tests.trigger: With a workspace slug='dup-ws' already existing in the workspaces table, send POST /api/v1/workspaces with slug='dup-ws' and a valid non-admin API key.
real_components: Workspace store (CreateWorkspace function, duplicate-slug error handling)
SQLite database (workspaces table with pre-existing slug='dup-ws')
expected_effects: HTTP 409 response with body: {"error": {"code": "409", "message": "workspace slug already exists"}}
No new row is inserted into the workspaces table
The existing row with slug='dup-ws' remains unmodified

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for admin token rejection: POST /api/v1/workspaces authenticated with an admin token returns HTTP 403 with the appropriate error envelope.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send POST /api/v1/workspaces with an admin token in the Authorization header and a valid workspace JSON body.
expected_effects: HTTP 403 response with body: {"error": {"code": "403", "message": "workspace creation requires user authentication"}}
No workspace row is inserted into the workspaces table
The workspace store CreateWorkspace function is never invoked
